title: paracroton integrifolius
intro: Paracroton integrifolius, also known as the bitter leaf or bitter bush, is a plant species in the family Euphorbiaceae. It is traditionally used in West African medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant's leaves and roots are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, offering several health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving immune function. In traditional herbal preparations, the leaves and roots are often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be consumed fresh or dried.
